subject: Databases
title: Unique Constraints
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Unique Constraints.

hook.text: Two users with the same email? A UNIQUE constraint on the email column prevents that entirely.

buildup.text: UNIQUE tells the database: no two rows can have the same value in this column (or combination of columns).

discovery.text: Composite unique constraints work too: UNIQUE(user_id, course_id) means one enrollment per user per course.

twist.text: NULLs are tricky. In most databases, multiple NULLs are allowed in a UNIQUE column. NULL isn't equal to NULL.

climax.text: UNIQUE creates an index automatically. It speeds up lookups on that column for free.

punchline.text: UNIQUE prevents duplicates and creates an index. Two benefits, one constraint.

quiz.question: Why are multiple NULLs typically allowed in a UNIQUE column?
options: NULL is not equal to NULL in SQL, so the uniqueness check doesn't apply
UNIQUE constraints ignore NULL values entirely
Only the first NULL is stored; others are converted to zero
